title: Tanker Shipping - June 30, 2026
summary: The VLCC spot rate in the Arabian Gulf has dropped from recent highs, driven by geopolitical risks and weakening Chinese demand, which has fallen to 6.4 million barrels per day. Despite global inventories remaining drawn down, crude prices have returned to pre-war levels due to a lack of appetite from Chinese refiners. Longer-term market outlook suggests a potential industry downcycle due to increased vessel ordering.

children.title: Overview
children.summary:
- VLCC Spot Drops from Crisis Highs, but Volatility Remains - The Very Large
- Oil Prices Drop to pre-war Levels as Oversupply becomes the Narrative - A
- Our Long-term View - The tanker market has been recovering from a long period
